name: Río Jabalón — circular desde Granátula de Calatrava
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Granátula De Calatrava navigate a landscape characterized by gently rolling terrain and the presence of the Jabalón River and its associated reservoirs. The region features open agricultural areas interspersed with low hills, offering varied cycling experiences. Elevation gains are generally moderate, making the area accessible for different skill levels of touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Granátula De Calatrava The most popular touring cycling route is Presa de Jabalón — circular desde Almagro, a 30.2 miles (48.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete, offering views of the Jabalón Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Granátula de Calatrava y Moral de Calatrava — circular desde Almagro, a moderate 28.1 miles (45.2 km) path. This route connects two towns through the regional landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Aldea del Rey - Jabalón Reservoir Loop, a 23.4 miles (37.6 km) trail leading through the area surrounding the Jabalón Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 5 minutes. Touring cycling around Granátula De Calatrava is defined by river valleys, reservoir views, and open countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy routes to more challenging ascents. The routes in Granátula De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 80 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Granátula De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Granátula De Calatrava?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes in the Granátula De Calatrava region, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Granátula De Calatrava?
A: Yes, the region features 2 easy touring cycling routes. A good option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058490607/rio-jabalon-circular-desde-granatula-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Jabalón — circular desde Granátula de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;, which is 15.2 miles (24.5 km) long with moderate elevation.

Q: What are the typical distances and elevation gains for touring cycling routes in this area?
A: Routes vary, but many are moderate, ranging from 20 to 35 miles (32 to 56 km) with elevation gains typically between 200 and 300 meters. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058505797/presa-de-jabalon-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Jabalón — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt; is 30.2 miles (48.6 km) with 264 meters of elevation gain. The terrain is characterized by gently rolling hills.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Granátula De Calatrava?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056883816/granatula-de-calatrava-y-moral-de-calatrava-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granátula de Calatrava y Moral de Calatrava — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a loop through the regional landscape.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on touring cycling routes in Granátula De Calatrava?
A: The routes offer diverse scenery, including views of the Jabalón River and its reservoirs, open agricultural areas, and low hills. You'll experience the characteristic landscape of the Calatrava region.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along the routes, you can explore various historical and natural attractions. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2895336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almagro Main Square&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2713622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oreto and Zuqueca Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3793255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervideros Baños del Barranco&lt;/a&gt; natural monument. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052859490/aldea-del-rey-jabalon-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldea del Rey - Jabalón Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes through areas with such points of interest.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Granátula De Calatrava?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic reservoir views, and the network of routes suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for more experienced riders, there are 7 difficult routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Calzada de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 33.8 miles (54.4 km) with significant elevation gain.

Q: Is Granátula De Calatrava suitable for family touring cycling trips?
A: Given the presence of easy and moderate routes with generally gentle terrain, Granátula De Calatrava can be suitable for families. It's recommended to choose the easier, shorter circular routes to ensure an enjoyable experience for all family members.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Granátula De Calatrava?
A: While not explicitly stated in the guide, the region's climate suggests that spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, avoiding the extreme heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

Q: Where can I find parking when starting a touring cycling route in Granátula De Calatrava?
A: Parking is generally available in the main towns where routes often begin or pass through, such as Granátula de Calatrava itself or nearby Almagro. Look for public parking areas within these towns.
tips:
The archaeological importance of the environment in which the site is located has been well known since ancient times, since the Cerro de Oreto is located nearby, which contains the ruins of the capital of Oretania, Oretum according to sources, (a Roman province that included from north to south beyond Sierra Morena and extended to the end of the Bética); the Cerro de los Obispos, an important Iberian site on whose slopes appeared some burials attributed to Visigothic bishops (one of them Bishop Amador, as recorded on the tombstone that covered his tomb); several late-Roman necropolises corresponding to that classical city and the Roman bridge over the Jabalón ordered to be built by Publio Baebio Venusto, dated between the end of the 1st century and the beginning of the 2nd century AD, and which has currently been covered by the waters of the Reservoir.

Numerous pieces belonging to different periods have been found, among which are coins, ceramics, farm elements, and loom stones.
Museumized volcano of Campo de Calatrava. The last eruption of the volcano was 700,000 years ago, it has a cone of 300 hectares and the lava flows reached 3 kilometers.
